source_metadata.title_raw: Jimmy Paulding - April 12, 2019 - A-19-053 - Arroyo Grande

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322 ~5660 • Fax (916 ) 322-0886 
Jimmy Paulding 
Arroyo Grande City Councilmember 
300 E. Branch Street 
Arroyo Grande, CA 93420 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-053 
Dear Mr. Paulding: 
April 12, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 • Please note that we are only providing advice under the 
conflict of interest provisions of Act, and Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest 
prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest. 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice -we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Luis Obispo 
County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097 .1 ( c )(3 ). ) We did not receive 
a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you 
that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.l(c)(S).) 
QUESTION 
Given that your spouse is a volunteer for the Five Cities Diversity Coalition ("FCDC"), a 
501(c)(3) non-profit organization, do you have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act or 
Section '1090 that prohibits you from taking part in a City Council decision to approve the 
allocation of a grant to FCDC? 
CONCLUSION 
No. Because your spouse does not receive income from FCDC, no financial conflict of 
interest is presented under the Act. With regard to Section 1090, your spouse is a noncompensated 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 ofthe California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-19-053 
Page No. 2 
officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, which, as one of its primary purposes, supports the 
functions of the City Council. Accordingly, you have a "noninterest" in FCDC under Section 
1091.5 and may take part in the "contracting process" of approving the grant, so long as your 
noninterest is noted in the City Council's records. 
FACTS 
You are a member of the Arroyo Grande City Council. Your spouse sits on the Sponsorship 
Executive Committee and Board of the Five Cities Diversity Coalition ("FCDC"), a 50l(c)(3) non-
profit organization. You have stated that FCDC' s mission is "to provide programs and resources 
promoting the inclusion and affirmation of diversity in our community and counter[] any negative 
acts of prejudice or bias against its citizens." Your spouse serves in her roles in an unpaid capacity. 
The City has a grant program intended to assist local non-profit organizations implement 
various public service programs that directly benefit Arroyo Grande residents. FCDC applied for a 
grant through the program. According to a memorandum attached to your request for advice, the 
grant program provides a funding opportunity for local public service programs that address unmet 
needs in the community. A six-member Community Service Grant Review Panel was established 
consisting of one member from each of the City's current and former boards, commissions, and 
committees. The panel reviewed the applications and provided funding recommendations for the 
City Council's approval, including a $2,500 grant to FCDC. The recommendations were primarily 
based on funding projects and programs that directly assist individuals having the most critical 
need. The City Council is tasked with approving or rejecting the recommended grant allocations. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act 
Under Section 87100 of the Act, "[ n ]o public official at any level of state or local 
government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to 
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial 
interest." 
Section 87103 provides that a public official has a financial interest in a decision within the 
meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of 
his or her immediate family, or certain enumerated economic interests. These economic interests 
include, for instance, a business entity in which the official has a direct or indirect investment of 
$2,000 or more, or any source of income aggregating $500 or more in value within the 12 months 
preceding the governmental decision at issue. (Section 87103(a), (c).) "Income," within the 
meaning of the Act, is defined as, "a payment received, including but not limited to any salary, 
wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sale, gift, including any gift of food or 
beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received by the filer, reimbursement for 
expenses, per diem, or contribution to an insurance or pension program paid by any person other 
than an employer, and including any community property interest in the income of a spouse." 
(Section 82030(a).) 


File No. A-19-053 
Page No. 3 
You have indicated that your spouse is "unpaid" for the services she provides to FCDC. If 
your spouse has not received any income (as listed above) amounting to $500 or more over the 
course of the year preceding the City Council's decision on the allocation of grants, then FCDC 
does not constitute a "source of income" to you under the Act. Accordingly, you spouse's volunteer 
work would not present a disqualifying financial conflict-of-interest and the Act would not prohibit 
you from taking part in the governmental decision. 
Section 1090 
Section 1090 generally prohibits public officers, while acting in their official capacities, 
from making contracts in which they are financially interested. Section 1090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote or minimal interests, that prevent public officials from 
exercising absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their 
agencies. (Stigall v. Taft (l 962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) Section 1090 is intended "not only to strike at 
actual impropriety, but also to strike at the appearance of impropriety." ( City of Imperial Beach v. 
Bailey (1980) 103 Cal.App.3d 191, 197.) 
Section 1091.5 sets forth various statutory categories of "noninterests" under Section 1090. 
If an official has only a "noninterest," the official in question may participate in the contract and 
disclosure is generally not necessary. (City of Vernon v. Central Basin Mun. Water Dist. (1999) 69 
Cal.App.4th 508, 514-515; 84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 159-160 (2001).) Section 1091.5(a)(8) 
provides that a "noninterest" includes: 
That of a non-compensated officer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, which, as 
one of its primary purposes, supports the functions of the body or board or to which 
the body or board has a legal obligation to give particular consideration, and 
provided further that this interest is noted in its official records. 
As noted, FCDC is a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation. As a volunteer FCDC board 
member, your wife qualifies as a non-compensated officer. Accordingly, the determinative question 
is whether one of FCDC's primary purposes supports the functions of the City. FCDC's mission is 
"to provide programs and resources promoting the inclusion and affirmation of diversity in our 
community and countering any negative acts of prejudice or bias against its citizens." The City also 
has an interest in achieving that mission statement, as evidenced by the fact that the grant program 
is specifically intended to provide local public service programs with funding to "address unmet 
needs in the community," and the Community Service Grant Review Panel has recommended grant 
funding for FCDC with that in mind. Accordingly, FCDC's primary purpose supports the functions 
of the City and you have a "noninterest" in FCDC, so long as your spouse's involvement with the 
organization remains on a volunteer basis. As long as your noninterest is noted in the City Council's 
official records, Section 1090 does not prohibit you from taking part in approving the allocation of a 
grant to FCDC. 


File No. A-19-053 
Page No. 4 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
KMC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Kevin Cornwall · 
Counsel, Legal Division

QUESTION: Given that your spouse is a volunteer for the Five Cities Diversity Coalition ("FCDC"), a 
501(c)(3) non-profit organization, do you have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act or 
Section '1090 that prohibits you from taking part in a City Council decision to approve the 
allocation of a grant to FCDC?

CONCLUSION: No. Because your spouse does not receive income from FCDC, no financial conflict of 
interest is presented under the Act. With regard to Section 1090, your spouse is a noncompensatedofficer of a nonprofit, tax-exempt corporation, which, as one of its primary purposes, supports the 
functions of the City Council. Accordingly, you have a "noninterest" in FCDC under Section 
1091.5 and may take part in the "contracting process" of approving the grant, so long as your 
noninterest is noted in the City Council's records.
